query: Draft a civil suit for recovery of possession of immovable property. Plaintiff is owner of the property and Defendant is in unauthorized occupation after expiry of permissive possession/lease. Defendant refusing to vacate despite legal notice. Seek recovery of possession, mesne profits, and costs.

pipeline.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{CITY}}, {{STATE}}

O.S. No. {{YEAR}} OF {{YEAR}}

BETWEEN:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Age: {{AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{ADDRESS}},

... PLAINTIFF

AND:

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Age: {{AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{ADDRESS}},

... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF POSSESSION OF IMMOVABLE PROPERTY AND MESNE PROFITS

The Plaintiff submits as follows:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has the jurisdiction to try and entertain this suit as the immovable property described in the Schedule of Property hereunder is situated within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court. The cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of this Court where the Defendant is in unauthorized occupation of the Plaintiff's property.

FACTS OF THE CASE

2. The Plaintiff is the absolute owner and landlord of the immovable property more particularly described in the Schedule of Property appended to this plaint. The Plaintiff holds clear title to the property evidenced by the Title Deed.

3. The Defendant was inducted into possession of the suit property by the Plaintiff as a tenant under a lease agreement / permissive possession. The Defendant's entry into the property was lawful at the inception based on the agreement between the parties.

4. The period of the permissive possession / lease granted to the Defendant has since expired. Upon the expiry of the said period, the Defendant's right to remain in possession ceased automatically.

5. The tenancy / permissive possession stands determined under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882. The Defendant has become an erstwhile tenant whose tenancy has been lawfully determined.

6. After the expiry of the lease period, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant calling upon them to vacate and hand over peaceful possession of the suit property. The Defendant received the said legal notice.

7. Despite receipt of the legal notice and the determination of the tenancy, the Defendant has refused to vacate the suit property. The Defendant is continuing in unauthorized occupation of the property without any lawful authority.

8. The Plaintiff has clear title and the Defendant's occupation is unauthorized. The Defendant has ignored the legal notice served upon them.

LEGAL BASIS

9. This suit is filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which mandates that every suit shall be instituted by the presentation of a plaint. The particulars of this plaint comply with Order VII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

10. The Plaintiff seeks recovery of possession under Section 5 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963. This provision entitles a person entitled to the possession of specific immovable property to recover it in the manner provided by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The Plaintiff is entitled to possession as the owner and landlord after determination of the tenancy.

11. The lease / permissive possession is governed by the Transfer of Property Act, 1882. Under Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, the lease is deemed to be month-to-month unless otherwise agreed, and under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, a lease of immovable property determines by efflux of the time limited thereby. The facts trigger these provisions as the lease period has expired.

12. The claim for mesne profits is founded on Section 2(12) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which defines mesne profits as those profits which the person in wrongful possession actually received or might with ordinary diligence have received, together with interest thereon. The Defendant is in wrongful possession day to day.

13. The decree for mesne profits is sought under Order XX Rule 12 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which allows the Court to pass a decree for possession along with an inquiry for mesne profits.

CAUSE OF ACTION

14. The cause of action for this suit first arose on {{DATE_OF_EXPIRY}} when the period of permissive possession / lease expired and the Defendant failed to vacate the property.

15. The cause of action is continuing in nature. The Defendant continues to be in wrongful possession day to day, causing ongoing loss to the Plaintiff. The cause of action continues to arise anew each day the Defendant remains in unauthorized occupation.

LIMITATION

16. This suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 67 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. This Article provides a period of twelve years for a suit by a landlord to recover possession from a tenant. The limitation period accrues when the tenancy is determined / lease expires. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

17. For the purpose of court fees and jurisdiction, the suit is valued at {{VALUATION_AMOUNT}}. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on this plaint.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

18. The suit property is described as follows:
All that piece and parcel of the immovable property bearing {{PROPERTY_DETAILS}}, situated at {{LOCATION}}, within the registration district of {{DISTRICT}}, bounded on the East by {{EAST_BOUND}}, West by {{WEST_BOUND}}, North by {{NORTH_BOUND}}, and South by {{SOUTH_BOUND}}.

MESNE PROFITS

19. The Plaintiff claims mesne profits for the period of wrongful occupation by the Defendant.
(a) Past Mesne Profits: The Plaintiff claims past mesne profits accrued from {{DATE_OF_EXPIRY}} till the date of filing of the suit. The specific amount is quantified at {{AMOUNT}} OR alternatively, an inquiry be directed to ascertain the actual amount due.
(b) Future Mesne Profits: The Plaintiff claims future mesne profits from the date of filing of the suit until the date of delivery of possession. The Plaintiff prays for an inquiry under Order XX Rule 12(1)(c) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 to ascertain the amount payable for the period during which the Defendant remains in wrongful possession after the suit filing.
(c) The mesne profits are claimed at the rate of {{RATE}} per month OR as determined by the Court upon inquiry based on the market rent of the property.

PRAYER

20. It is therefore prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:
(a) Pass a decree for recovery of possession of the immovable property described in the Schedule of Property in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant;
(b) Pass a preliminary decree for mesne profits under Order XX Rule 12(1) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 directing an inquiry into:
(i) Past mesne profits accrued from {{DATE_OF_EXPIRY}} till the date of filing of the suit;
(ii) Future mesne profits from the date of filing of the suit till delivery of possession;
(c) Pass a final decree for the amount found due upon such inquiry;
(d) Award interest on the mesne profits as per law;
(e) Award the costs of this suit to the Plaintiff; and
(f) Pass any other order or relief that this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

21. The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:
(a) Legal notice issued to Defendant to vacate;
(b) Property ownership documents (Title Deed);
(c) Lease agreement or proof of permissive possession.

VERIFICATION

22. Verified at {{PLACE}} on this {{DAY}} day of {{MONTH}}, {{YEAR}}.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby declare that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 21 of the plaint are true and correct to my knowledge and belief. No part of it is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified by the Plaintiff.

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
(Plaintiff)

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF:

{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
{{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT_NUMBER}}
{{OFFICE_ADDRESS}}
{{CONTACT_DETAILS}}

direct.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE SENIOR CIVIL JUDGE (SENIOR DIVISION) AT {{CITY/DISTRICT}}, {{STATE}}

CIVIL SUIT NO. {{NUMBER}} OF {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:

{{PLAINTIFF NAME}}, S/O {{FATHER'S NAME}}, AGED ABOUT {{AGE}} YEARS, R/O {{FULL ADDRESS}} ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT NAME}}, S/O {{FATHER'S NAME}}, AGED ABOUT {{AGE}} YEARS, R/O {{FULL ADDRESS}} ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF POSSESSION OF IMMOVABLE PROPERTY, MESNE PROFITS, AND COSTS

BODY OF THE PLAINT

1. The Plaintiff states that he is the absolute owner and person in lawful possession of the immovable property bearing Survey No. {{NUMBER}}, Khasra No. {{NUMBER}}, situated at {{LOCATION}}, admeasuring {{AREA}} (hereinafter referred to as the "Suit Property"). The Plaintiff derives title from the Registered Sale Deed dated {{DATE}}, registered as Document No. {{NUMBER}} in the office of the Sub-Registrar at {{LOCATION}}. The mutation of the Suit Property stands in the name of the Plaintiff in the Revenue Records since {{DATE}}.

2. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant was initially allowed to occupy the Suit Property on a leave and license basis/tenancy basis for a fixed period commencing from {{START DATE}} and ending on {{END DATE}}. The terms of the agreement expressly stipulated that upon expiry of the said period, the Defendant was bound to vacate and hand over peaceful possession to the Plaintiff.

3. The Plaintiff states that the agreed period of permissive possession/lease expired on {{END DATE}}. The Plaintiff duly informed the Defendant regarding the expiry of the term and requested him to vacate the Suit Property peacefully. However, the Defendant has failed and refused to vacate the premises.

4. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant's occupation of the Suit Property subsequent to {{END DATE}} is wholly unauthorized, illegal, and without any lawful title. The Defendant has become a trespasser in the eye of the law since the expiry of the agreed period. The Plaintiff has not received any rent or license fee from the Defendant since {{DATE}}.

5. The Plaintiff states that in order to comply with the statutory requirements and to put the Defendant on notice, the Plaintiff issued a Legal Notice through his Advocate dated {{DATE}} via Registered Post with Acknowledgement Due. The said notice was duly served upon the Defendant on {{DATE}}, as evidenced by the Postal Acknowledgement Card.

6. The Plaintiff states that despite receipt of the Legal Notice, the Defendant sent a frivolous reply dated {{DATE}} denying the Plaintiff's title and refusing to vacate the Suit Property. The contentions raised by the Defendant in his reply are baseless, false, and designed to delay the proceedings. The Defendant has no lawful right to remain in possession of the Suit Property.

7. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant is liable to pay mesne profits for the unauthorized use and occupation of the Suit Property from {{DATE}} till the date of actual handing over of possession. The mesne profits are tentatively calculated at the rate of {{AMOUNT}} per month, totaling {{TOTAL AMOUNT}} till the date of filing of this suit.

8. The Plaintiff states that the cause of action for the present suit first arose on {{DATE}} when the lease/license agreement expired and the Defendant failed to vacate. The cause of action further arose on {{DATE}} when the Legal Notice was served upon the Defendant and subsequently refused/complied with falsely. The cause of action is continuing till date as the Defendant is still in illegal possession.

9. The Plaintiff states that the Suit Property is situated within the territorial limits of {{CITY/DISTRICT}}, and hence this Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

10. The Plaintiff states that the value of the Suit Property for the purpose of court fee and jurisdiction is assessed at {{AMOUNT}}. The requisite court fee has been paid on the plaint as per the provisions of the {{STATE}} Court Fees Act. The Plaintiff further values the claim for mesne profits at {{AMOUNT}} for the purpose of court fee.

11. The Plaintiff states that the present suit is filed within the period of limitation as prescribed under the Limitation Act, 1963. The suit is barred by no law and is maintainable in its present form.

12. The Plaintiff states that there is no other suit or proceeding pending between the parties regarding the subject matter of this suit in any Court of Law. The Plaintiff has not previously filed any suit which has been dismissed or returned for presentation.

13. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant is attempting to create third-party interests in the Suit Property by way of unauthorized subletting or encumbrance, which requires a decree of permanent injunction to restrain the Defendant from alienating the Suit Property during the pendency of this suit.

14. The Plaintiff therefore prays for a judgment and decree in his favor and against the Defendant for the following reliefs:
(A) A decree for recovery of possession of the Suit Property described in the Schedule below;
(B) A decree for mesne profits at the rate of {{AMOUNT}} per month from {{DATE}} till the date of actual possession, along with interest @ {{RATE}}% per annum;
(C) A decree for permanent injunction restraining the Defendant from interfering with the Plaintiff's peaceful possession after recovery;
(D) Costs of the suit; and
(E) Any other relief that this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

All that piece and parcel of the immovable property bearing Survey No. {{NUMBER}}, Khasra No. {{NUMBER}}, situated at {{LOCATION}}, bounded by:
North: {{DETAILS}}
South: {{DETAILS}}
East: {{DETAILS}}
West: {{DETAILS}}
Admeasuring {{AREA}}.

VERIFICATION

15. I, {{PLAINTIFF NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify and declare that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 14 of the plaint are true and correct to my knowledge and belief and based on legal advice received. The last paragraph is the prayer to this Hon'ble Court. Verified at {{CITY}} on this {{DATE}} day of {{MONTH}}, {{YEAR}}.

PLAINTIFF

THROUGH COUNSEL:

{{ADVOCATE NAME}}
ADVOCATE
ENROLLMENT NO. {{NUMBER}}
CHAMBER NO. {{NUMBER}}, {{BAR ASSOCIATION BUILDING}}
{{CITY}}, {{STATE}} - {{PIN CODE}}
MOBILE: {{NUMBER}}
EMAIL: {{EMAIL}}

LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON

1. Copy of Registered Sale Deed dated {{DATE}}.
2. Copy of Mutation Extract.
3. Copy of Lease/Leave and License Agreement dated {{DATE}}.
4. Copy of Legal Notice dated {{DATE}}.
5. Copy of Postal Acknowledgement Card.
6. Copy of Reply to Legal Notice (if any).
7. Power of Attorney/Vakalatnama.

END OF PLAINT
